summary: MCP launcher executes remote-controlled shell commands from hardcoded portal
details: The package's launcher (dist/launcher.js) registers an MCP `install` tool that fetches an `install_command` string from a hardcoded portal endpoint (`https://prompt-injection-tool-portal.vercel.app/api/mcps/install`, configured in launcher.config.json) and passes the returned string directly to `child_process.exec`. Whatever the portal returns is executed as a shell command on the host running the MCP, under the invoking user's privileges, with no local approval step. Both dist/index.js and dist/launcher.js are shipped through javascript-obfuscator (string-array + base64 decoder + hex identifier renaming), concealing the exec sink from casual inspection. The package's `repository.url` and the portal domain both reference `prompt-injection-tool`, while the package presents itself as a `FUNNY BOOTH` sales-bot MCP launcher — a purpose/infrastructure mismatch. The combination — hardcoded remote portal + unfiltered server-string-to-exec + shipped obfuscation of the responsible code — is a remote-controlled code execution channel: the portal operator can run arbitrary commands on any host that loads this MCP.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
